record.field: Valkey is a high-performance, open source in-memory key-value data store — a community-driven, drop-in replacement for Redis for caching, sessions and queues.

Valkey is a high-performance, open source in-memory key-value data store — a community-driven, drop-in replacement for Redis for caching, sessions and queues.

Forked from Redis 7.2 and stewarded by the Linux Foundation, Valkey keeps full protocol and API compatibility so existing Redis clients and tooling work unchanged. It is aimed at developers and platform teams that need a fast, reliable data layer for caching, session storage, rate limiting, lightweight queues and high-throughput key/value workloads, with a truly open license and an active community behind it.

Key features
  • Drop-in Redis compatibility — existing clients, commands and data structures work unchanged
  • Rich data types: strings, hashes, lists, sets, sorted sets, streams and more
  • Durable persistence with append-only file (AOF) and RDB snapshots
  • Password authentication enforced by default for a hardened internal service
  • Sub-millisecond latency for caching, sessions, rate limiting and pub/sub
  • BSD-licensed, vendor-neutral project governed under the Linux Foundation
Best for

Teams that need a fast, self-hosted cache or in-memory store as a shared dependency for their applications, and anyone looking for an openly licensed community alternative to Redis. Deployed as an internal-only service reachable by other apps on the private network.

services: valkey: image: valkey/valkey:8 command: ["valkey-server", "--appendonly", "yes", "--requirepass", "{{ valkey_password }}"] environment: - VALKEY_PASSWORD={{ valkey_password }} volumes: - ./valkey_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped expose: - 6379 networks: - internal healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "valkey-cli -a \"$$VALKEY_PASSWORD\" ping | grep -q PONG"] interval: 30s timeout: 5s retries: 5 networks: internal: driver: bridge
Password required to authenticate clients against Valkey (requirepass).
